key_insights.insight: Small wins can be incredibly meaningful, especially when coming from a place of hardship.
key_insights.quote: Small wins or little wins in my life were massive... a hug from my mum that felt a little bit like a parental hug rather than a needy or an angry or, that would be a huge, like, I'd done out on that for a month.

key_insights.insight: Admitting powerlessness and surrendering to a support system is crucial for overcoming addiction.
key_insights.quote: I don't care what I have to do I will literally do anything to stop feeling like this and NA taught me how to live and how to change and how to heal myself.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes professional goals aimed at external validation will ultimately leave you feeling unfulfilled.
key_insights.quote: It hasn't fixed the hole it it didn't make me think oh my mum's gonna want me back

key_insights.insight: It's essential to act on your manifestations and proactively pursue opportunities, and to take the first step even if it seems rude.
key_insights.quote: If you just set the sat-nav, you're going to be in your garage all day; you have to do both together... be as annoying as a mosquito.

key_insights.insight: Confrontation should not be avoided; it might be the only solution to problems.
key_insights.quote: After a few years of being in recovery, talking to my sponsor, going to meetings, sharing about it, I thought I'm going to confront her about it. And I said, you're an alcoholic. You need to do something about it.

key_insights.insight: Defining what truly matters and letting go of toxicity should be done now, not after a health scare.
key_insights.quote: Don't wait for somebody to say that you've got six weeks to live, say I love, I say I love you to all of my friends all of the people that I love non-stop, check in with people, call people, make sure they're okay, spend time with people... if you've got somebody very toxic in your life, the first thing I'd do is let go of this toxic person.

key_insights.insight: Forgiveness, even without reconciliation, brings peace and the ability to move forward.
key_insights.quote: I just kept saying, I forgive you for everything. I just totally fucking forgive you. I don't care anymore. Forgive you. Go in peace.

key_insights.insight: Positivity can be learned, by replacing negative sentences with 'but luckily'
key_insights.quote: If you are a negative person at the end of, you know, it's raining and it's rained for the third day in a row, you finish your negative sentence with, but luckily.

actionable_advice.advice: When struggling with negative thoughts, complete the sentence with "but luckily" and find a positive aspect to the situation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Consciously force yourself to find a positive consequence or perspective even when feeling negative. Repeat for every negative thought for 2-3 weeks.

actionable_advice.advice: Don't wait for a life-altering event to change your priorities; take action now.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify toxic relationships or unfulfilling aspects of your life and actively remove them.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice self-care, even in small ways, by focusing on your internal comfort and confidence.
actionable_advice.how_to: Choose clothes that make YOU feel good, even if no one else sees them, as an act of self-love.

actionable_advice.advice: Speak your truth and be honest.
actionable_advice.how_to: Confront someone if you think they are acting badly, or if they hurt you. You might help them or change their lives, but in the worst case, you'll still be satisfied that you've said it.

memorable_stories: Davina's story of being abandoned by her mother at her grandmother's house.
Davina's anecdotes of doing drugs with her mother and sister.
Davina's account of her sister Caroline's cancer diagnosis and their final weeks together, including Caroline's refusal to show skin before finally accepting help.
Davina's description of her hypnosis session and the breakthrough regarding her fear of abandonment.
The story of the guy who got Davina's menopause book and the threw it in the lounge and ran away.
contrarian_claims: That canceling someone doesn't let somebody learn something and you gotta let them learn the lesson.
The speaker disagrees with the blanket condemnation of all men following Sarah Everard's death and subsequent outrage.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Davina McCall: “I was doing cocaine with my mum at 15!”
